other_observers: Dix Smith and Mr. and Mrs. Barnes;
specific_location: Prewitt Reservoir, near the boat ramp.
description: The bird was clearly of jaeger shape. It was generally dark but heavy patterning and barring was evident on its flanks and interior underwing. Its size was close to or smaller than a Ring-billed Gull. The central retrices extensions were long and rounded and the upper wing showed limited white on the outer primaries.Behaviors: The bird circled the observers area at close range several times then flew off a couple hundred yards to shore where it landed close to a fisherman. It was not seen harassing gulls or otherwise feeding during the approximate 5 minutes it was seen.Call: N/APlumage: Dark morph juvenile
